info.title: Trackdesk public API
info.description:
Tenant ID
=========

Please, fill in your tenant ID, a.k.a. the subdomain on which is your program running.
In case of https://my-awesome-affiliate-system.trackdesk.com, your tenant ID is my-awesome-affiliate-system

API key
=======

Please, fill in your API key from your settings.

How to get an API key?
=================

1. Navigate to your program URL, e.g. https://my-awesome-affiliate-system.trackdesk.com.
2. Click on your name at the right top corner.
3. Click on Settings.
4. Click on Personal access tokens in Integrations section.
5. Generate new API key and copy its content to the input above.

post.description:
Creates a new note attached to a specific affiliate account.
- Errors: may use ERROR_CODE_ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND when account_id does not exist.

requestBody.description: Request to create a new note for an affiliate account.

get.description: Lists notes for a given affiliate account ordered by creation time.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Affiliate account UUID whose notes are requested.

delete.description: Removes a note by its unique identifier.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Note UUID to delete.

parameters.description: Day until which the open balance is computed. When omitted, the current time is used.

Updates the status of a single affiliate settlement.

 - PAYMENT_METHOD_TYPE_TIPALTI: WARNING: This enum index (16) is referenced in cel expression in registration.proto.

parameters.description: Filter rows by offers having at least one of these tag IDs assigned (OR semantics).

parameters.name: filter.revenueOriginTagIds
parameters.description: Filter rows by advertisers (revenue origins) having at least one of these tag IDs assigned (OR semantics).

post.description: Shows data related to conversions tracked by the system.

get.summary: List conversion types available for CID
get.description: Provides a list of conversion types that are available for the requesting click identifier (CID)
Create a new conversion in the system.

parameters.name: cid
parameters.description: The click identification associated with the conversion. When cid is filled, then external_cid/external_client_id cannot be filled.

parameters.name: externalCid
parameters.description:
External CID associated with the conversion. See [Conversion sources](#tag--tracking.v1.ConversionSourceService) for more details.

when external_cid is filled, then revenue_origin_id must also be filled, and cid/external_client_id cannot be filled

parameters.name: externalClientId
parameters.description:
External client ID associated with the conversion.

When external_client_id is filled, then revenue_origin_id must also be filled, and cid/external_cid cannot be filled

parameters.name: couponCodes
parameters.description:
Coupons to be redeemed within the conversion.

when coupon codes are not empty, then revenue_origin_id must also be filled

parameters.name: revenueOriginId
parameters.description: Assigned revenue origin. Can be obtained from unique trackdesk identifiers in the settings.

parameters.name: conversionTypeCode
parameters.description: Type of the conversion.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Status of the conversion.

parameters.name: currency.code
parameters.description: The three-letter currency code defined in ISO 4217.

parameters.name: externalId
parameters.description: External ID  is used for deduplication of conversions. When the conversion is created with an External ID, no other conversion can be created with the same External ID.

parameters.name: customerId
parameters.description: Customer ID, your specific custom identifier.

parameters.name: customParams.advS1
parameters.description: Custom adv S1 parameter assigned to a conversion.

parameters.name: customParams.advS2
parameters.description: Custom adv S2 parameter assigned to a conversion.

parameters.name: customParams.advS4

parameters.name: customParams.advS5
parameters.description: Custom adv S5 parameter assigned to a conversion.

parameters.name: qualified
parameters.description: Qualification baseline evaluation value - override default behavior.

parameters.name: createdAt
parameters.description: The date-time when the conversion was created. Must be in the past. Uses RFC 3339 formatting (e.g. "2036-07-21T17:32:28Z"). If not provided, the current time-stamp will be used.

get.summary: List payable account settlements of a settlement
get.description: Retrieve list of payable and unpaid account settlements via Tipalti.

parameters.name: settlementId
parameters.description: Unique ID of a settlement

post.description:
Sends new payment batch to Tipalti system for specified settlement.

Account settlements of the specified settlement are included in the payment batch only if it has Tipalti payment method, is still unpaid and
Tipalti payee corresponding to the account settlement's affiliate is in payable state.

parameters.name: settlementId
parameters.description: Unique ID of a settlement

post.description:
Checks status of a specific Tipalti payment batch of given settlement and refreshes the payment data of the settlement.
This should be used only if there is need to manually synchronize payment data with new state in Tipalti system.

You can then see the refreshed data in SettlementService.GetSettlement endpoint.

parameters.name: settlementId
parameters.description: Unique ID of a settlement

Rejection-specific details. Only honored when rejecting a pending affiliate registration
(transitioning from Pending to Disabled); ignored for any other status transition.

Optional rejection reason. Saved with the decision and injected into the rejection email
template as {rejectionReason} when non-empty.

sendEmail.description: Whether to send the rejection email to the affiliate.

billing.v1.AccountSettlement.PaymentData.description: TipaltiPaymentData holds details specific to Tipalti payments.

paymentInstructions.description:
List of payment instructions.

Each instruction represents a payment attempt made by Tipalti system.
For more information about the containing data you can check the Tipalti documentation: https://documentation.tipalti.com/reference/get_api-v1-payment-batches-id-instructions

paymentBatchId.description: Unique identifier for the payment batch.

payeeIsPayable.description: Indicates if the payee is payable.

errors.description:
List of errors in the payment instruction.

Provides details on errors within a payment instruction as received from Tipalti.
For more information you can check Tipalti documentation: https://documentation.tipalti.com/reference/http-status-codes#api-error-object

currencyCode.description: Currency code for the payment method.

settledBalance.description: Settled balance amounts grouped by currency.

billing.v1.Settlement.PaymentData.description: Payment data associated with the settlement.

billing.v1.Settlement.PaymentData.Tipalt.description: Tipalti payment data holds details specific to Tipalti payments.

paymentBatches.description:
List of payment batches.

PaymentBatch represents a batch of payments sent to Tipalti system.
For more information about the containing data you can check the Tipalti documentation: https://documentation.tipalti.com/reference/get_api-v1-payment-batches-id

paymentBatchId.description: Unique identifier for the payment batch.

isValidForSubmission.description: Indicates if the batch is valid for submission.

errors.description:
List of errors in the payment batch.

Error provides details on errors within a payment batch as received from Tipalti.
For more information you can check Tipalti documentation: https://documentation.tipalti.com/reference/http-status-codes#api-error-object

inspectionPossibleAfter.description: Time after which inspection is possible.

cid.description: Unique identifier of a click. This parameter is used when creating new conversions.

requestUrl.description: URL of the origin of the incoming click.

referer.description: URL of the referring page that directed the user to the tracking link.

cityLatLong.description: GPS coordinates of the click's origin.

linkId.description: ID of the link to which the click is assigned.

ipAddress.description: IP address from where the click originated.

countryCode.description:
Code of the country from where the click originated. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (two-letter) format.
If not provided, the whole geolocation data will be determined from the IP address.
In that case, subdivision_code, city, and city_lat_long must not be provided.

subdivisionCode.description: Subdivision of the country of origin. Uses ISO-3166-2 format. Must not be provided if country_code is not set.

city.description: City from where the click originated. Must not be provided if country_code is not set.

requestUrl.description: URL of the origin of the incoming click.

referer.description: URL of the referring page that directed the user to the tracking link.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the click was registered.

monetary.description: Whether the metric represents a monetary value.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

google.type.LatLng.description: An object that represents a latitude/longitude pair. This is expressed as a pair of doubles to represent degrees latitude and degrees longitude. Unless specified otherwise, this must conform to the WGS84 standard. Values must be within normalized ranges.

latitude.description: The latitude in degrees. It must be in the range [-90.0, +90.0].

longitude.description: The longitude in degrees. It must be in the range [-180.0, +180.0].
